title: No Name Provided's Easy Farming
description: Not satisfied with the vanilla farming experience? Neither was I - let's overhaul the hoe tiers and add in some farming utilities.
body:
![Frontal view of player, holding the handles of a plough. A trail of ploughed ground is behind the player and a horse is partially visible beside the camera.](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f937cc606323cf56246b3beb4f5824102380cc43_0.webp)
# What Does This Mod Do?

There are a bunch of mods that add one-block solutions for growing crops, but there aren't many mods that add utilities to make in-world farming fun. And vanilla is pretty lacking in this regard - when's the last time you crafted a netherite hoe? Or even a diamond one? Farming is a core Minecraft mechanic, but there just isn't much progression. This mod aims to change that - by crafting tools from advanced (vanilla) materials, like blaze rods and breeze rods, you'll be able to make hoes that are meaningful impovements over that first wooden hoe you made on day 1.

This mod includes a few utility items to fill in gaps and make landscaping easier, without breaking vanilla progression or unbalancing the game experience.

## Features:
- A variety of useful hoes
- A plough (don't forget to hook up a horse)
- Fluidscaping utilities
- A bag for your seeds (my favorite)
- Fertilizer for sugarcane and cactus
- A custom dimension (comes sowed)
- The ability to season food (configurable)
- Support for farming in Nether only runs

## The Hoes:
- Storm - vein mines crops
- Radiant - always illuminated, smokes drops
- Reinforced - never breaks
- Fertilizer - grows crops
- Void - a union of utilities

Install Patchouli and check the in game guide for additional information.

---
This mod was designed with compatiblity in mind. Most behavior is tag driven, or can be adjusted in the configs. I made a special effort to support Pam's HarvestCraft 2 and Farmer's Delight, but any properly tagged mod should be integrated automatically. The bottomless bucket is compatible with modded tanks, where it acts as an infinite water source/sink. I explicitly support JEI and implicitly support EMI (through its JEI compat).

Though the license is all rights are reserved, you should interpret that as "ask first". You may use this in any modpack so long as you do the basics: don't redistribute, give me credit anywhere you thank/credit mod authors, make an effort to resolve bugs before passing them along to me, etc.

Legitimate versions of this mod can only be obtained from CurseForge and Modrinth.
